title: Who's Looking at Hoo
description: A juvenile barn owl, right place, right light, right moment. No tricks. Just patience.
longDescription:
This is a juvenile barn owl, caught in a quiet moment just before dusk in the kind of dappled light that we get up in the morning for, or spend the afternoon waiting for. That heart-shaped face is one of nature's better designs, built to funnel sound as much as to see, and when one turns it directly at you, it stops you in your tracks.

I photograph wildlife the way I believe it should be done. No bait, no calls, no disturbance. I show up, I wait, and I let the moment come to me. I just happened by this one, took a couple of quick shots and moved on so as not to disturb this little one.

Barn owls are silent hunters and surprisingly private creatures. Finding one willing to hold still long enough to really be seen is a gift. I didn't take it for granted.

No owls were bothered in the making of this photograph.
tools: Tools: Sony a1 II & Sony FE 600mm f/4 G OSS
